query: I have a column chart of Sales Amount by Year. How do I let users click into a year to see quarters then months?
why: Classic drill-down along a date hierarchy on a chart axis — core target intent.

query: want users to click a product in my matrix and jump to a page showing every individual transaction line for that SKU
why: Summary-to-detail drill-through to a filtered detail page — core target intent.

query: On my retail dashboard I need to show inventory on hand right next to sales for the same product and store. They're in different fact tables.
why: Multi-fact comparison over conformed dimensions = drill-across; beats retail-analytics-visualization which only picks single visuals.

query: how do i set up a decomposition tree so people can explore profit by region then category then customer in any order
why: Decomposition tree used for free-order hierarchical drilling — explicitly in target scope; wins over ai-powered-analysis-selection because intent is drill navigation.

query: my drillthrough detail page is super slow when users click from the summary, takes 8+ seconds to filter the transactions
why: Drill-through performance is an explicit trigger keyword in the skill.

query: need a back button and breadcrumb so users can return to the overview after they dig into a customer's order detail
why: Back button / breadcrumbs are part of designing drill-through navigation.

query: users keep dead-ending at the summary numbers on the exec page and cant get to the underlying rows, how should i design the navigation
why: Describes the summary dead-end problem the skill solves without naming drill-through.

query: set up the Product Dept > Category > Brand > SKU hierarchy so the bar chart drill icons expand to the next level
why: Building a product hierarchy for built-in drill-down icons — core target intent.

query: i want to compare shipments fact and orders fact by the same Date and Customer dimension on one report — what's the right approach
why: Two fact tables sharing conformed dimensions = drill-across pattern selection.

query: should a report tooltip page that shows transaction details on hover count as drill, and how do i wire it to the clicked category
why: Report tooltips / detail-on-hover are listed triggers; uncommon-but-valid drill-pattern case.

query: how do i add a year/quarter/month date hierarchy to my model and mark the date table for time intelligence
why: Building/marking a date table in the model belongs to modeling-semantic-data, not drill navigation design.

query: I need a YTD vs prior-year YTD measure broken down by quarter for the finance report
why: Period-over-period YTD DAX belongs to applying-time-intelligence.

query: add slicers and a bookmark toggle so users can switch between the sales and margin views on my dashboard
why: Slicers/bookmarks/buttons interactivity is building-interactive-reports, not drill pattern selection.

query: which chart should i use to show sales contribution by category — treemap, pie, or stacked bar?
why: Chart-type selection for a business question is mapping-questions-to-charts (treemap overlaps but no drill intent).

query: my inventory snapshot is semi-additive — how do i aggregate on-hand quantity correctly so it doesn't sum across dates
why: Semi-additive aggregation handling is understanding-fact-additivity.

query: the matrix visual takes forever to render with 12 million transaction rows, how do i speed up the model
why: Large-model/render performance tuning is optimizing-model-performance, not drill-through design.

query: set cross-filter direction between the Sales fact and Product dimension so the relationship filters both ways
why: Relationship cardinality/cross-filter setup is modeling-semantic-data.

query: i want a Key Influencers visual to tell me what drives customer churn on the telecom dashboard
why: Key Influencers AI visual selection is ai-powered-analysis-selection.

query: build a pixel-perfect printable invoice in Report Builder that drills the customer's line items for billing
why: Pixel-perfect RDL invoice output is building-paginated-reports despite 'drills' wording.

query: how do i lay out the regional sales dashboard for ops managers — what goes top-left vs bottom and how many visuals
why: Multi-visual layout for an audience is dashboard-composition.
